market_context: Iran-US Strait of Hormuz crisis (oil at $126 intraday). Strong Q1 earnings from Alphabet, Eli Lilly, Caterpillar. Meta sold off on AI capex. Fed divided 8-4 on rates. GDP at 2.0% below estimates.

picks.thesis: Strait of Hormuz at 4% of normal flow (Goldman estimate). 20% of global oil/gas supply effectively shut. Trump rejected Iran's proposal to reopen. This isn't a one-day spike -- it's a sustained supply disruption with no resolution in sight. CVX trades at 3.8% dividend yield with massive free cash flow at $120+ oil. Previous cause-and-effect: CVX rallied from $155 to $188+ during Venezuela sanctions (Jan 2026), an 18% gain driven by the same supply-disruption thesis. Same pattern, bigger catalyst. Risk: sudden ceasefire or deal announcement.

Q1 revenue $109.9B (+22% YoY), crushing $107.1B estimate. Net income +81% to $62.6B. Google Cloud crossed $20B for first time (+63% growth). Backlog nearly doubled to $460B. The market is worried about $180-190B AI capex, but Google has the profit engine to fund it -- $62.6B net income in ONE quarter. The gap up to $370 creates new support at the prior resistance level. Cloud growth acceleration suggests AI monetization is working, unlike Meta where spending has no clear ROI path. Risk: broader tech selloff on AI spending fears.

GLP-1 revenue explosion: Mounjaro +125% to $8.66B, Zepbound +80% to $4.16B. EPS $8.55 vs $6.85 expected. Raised 2026 revenue outlook. This is not a one-quarter beat -- it's a structural shift in healthcare spending. Global obesity drug market projected $150B+ by 2030. Lilly has the manufacturing capacity others lack. The 9% post-earnings pop has room to continue as analysts raise price targets. Risk: GLP-1 safety concerns or competitive pressure from Novo Nordisk.
